data.title: Two Harbors Investment cut to Neutral at UBS on UWMC deal
data.description:
UBS analyst Doug Harter downgraded Two Harbors Investment (TWO) shares to Neutral from Buy in a recent note after mortgage lender UWMC Holdings (UWMC) said it's buying the REIT in a ~$1.3B all-stock deal.

Until the deal closes—which is expected in the second quarter—"we believe that TWO shares will trade primarily on deal dynamics, rather than fundamentals," Haier wrote in a note. He added that he doesn't see the transaction encountering any big regulatory challenges.

Two Harbors (TWO) stock has jumped 21% since the merger was announced in mid-December, compared with the S&P 500's 2.9% gain.

UBS's Neutral rate agrees with the average Wall Street analyst rating of Hold but diverges from the average SA analyst rating of Buy.

TWO shares were little changed in Friday late morning trading.

data.title: SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: GE, MSFT, SMCI, AMGN
data.description:
GE Aerospace (GE) receives a rating upgrade from Dhierin Bechai, who argues that valuation concerns based on P/E ratios are misleading, while Amgen (AMGN) earns a Buy rating from Edmund Ingham on the strength of its MariTide obesity drug potential. On the downgrade side, Deep Value Investing moves Microsoft (MSFT) to Hold ahead of earnings amid cloud margin pressures, and Analysis Fundamental downgrades Super Micro Computer (SMCI) to Hold citing persistent shipment delays and low profit margins.

Upgrades

  • GE Aerospace (GE): Upgrade Hold to Buy by Dhierin Bechai. The analyst corrected his valuation methodology, shifting from a conglomerate-era multiple to one reflecting GE Aerospace’s current business structure, revealing an 11% upside with a $346.31 price target.

    “When we look at GE Aerospace’s valuation, we see that the stock is currently fairly valued with 2027 earnings in mind using the EV/EBITDA for the peer group. That makes sense, as I believe the market should be forward-looking.”

  • Amgen (AMGN): Upgrade Hold to Buy by Edmund Ingham. The analyst cites the transformative potential of Amgen’s obesity drug candidate MariTide alongside robust pipeline prospects and a healthy 2.9% dividend yield.

    “If we remove Mari-Tide from the equation entirely, Amgen appears to be a fairly valued company, its P/E and P/S ratios about standard for the sector, its patent expiry woes offset by the strong performance of recently approved assets and pipeline prospects. … If we add the ‘MariTide factor,’ there is an opportunity for a major valuation / share price upward correction if data are positive and support approval.”

Downgrades

  • Microsoft (MSFT): Downgrade Buy to Hold by Deep Value Investing. The analyst expresses concern about declining cloud gross margins and believes increased CapEx guidance is no longer viewed positively by the market ahead of FQ2 2026 earnings.

    “With Cloud gross margins pressured (68% last quarter, guided to 66% in FQ2) and CapEx still ramping up, I see the risk of a cautionary margin guide for FQ3. I no longer believe that a hike in CapEx guidance is positive for the hyperscalers. … Unless management puts out a spectacular FQ3 growth guidance for Azure, back to 40% YOY, I don’t see shares recovering from the recent selloff.”

  • Super Micro Computer, Inc. (SMCI): Downgrade Buy to Hold by Analysis Fundamental. The analyst highlights persistent shipment delays, a volatile operational model, and weak pricing power as key concerns despite strong AI infrastructure demand.

    “Growth catalysts of advanced cooling systems, partnership with NVDA, reduced time-to-market via DCBBS solutions and global expansion, still can’t overcome the company’s low margins and volatile operational model. … I downgrade SMCI stock to Hold, mainly due to the persistence of shipment delays and generally its volatile operational model. Additionally, I would like to see higher margins before initiating a position.”

data.title: FormFactor falls amid new short call at Hedgeye
data.description:
FormFactor (FORM) dropped 2.1% at least partly amid a new short idea from Hedgeye, which sees the potential for 45% downside. 

"FORM shares have more than tripled from cycle lows and now trade at ~50x blended 2026/2027 earnings—an extreme valuation that leaves no margin for error," Hedgeye analyst Felix Wang wrote in a note on Friday. "In our view, virtually all good news is priced in."

After Intel's "sharp guide-down" on Thursday, Hedgeye is reassessing hedges in the "overheated" semiconductor capital equipment space. FormFactor (FORM) screens as a compelling short, Wang wrote.

"Buyside expectations are skewed toward a significant beat-and-raise on February 4th, driven by assumed HBM/DRAM and advanced packaging strength (TSMC)," Hedgeye's Wang added. "Intel’s commentary meaningfully undermines that setup. A modest or nonexistent guidance raise would likely disappoint consensus."

"With stretched valuation, fading HBM tailwinds, ongoing share losses, and elevated expectations into earnings, FORM represents an asymmetric short," Wang wrote. "We estimate ~45% downside to fair value."

data.title: Best performing chip stocks in the last 12 months
data.description:
As Intel's (INTC) weaker-than-expected outlook and supply constraints continued to pull down the shares of the company on Friday, we took a look at the top performing chip-related stocks in the last twelve months.

Below is a list of the top 10 stocks in the Semiconductors and Semiconductor Materials & Equipment sectors ranked according to their 12-month price performance.

The list is topped by Micron Technology (MU), with a 12-month performance of 263.98% and a Strong Buy quant rating of 4.99. Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS) and ACM Research (ACMR) follow in second and third place, with gains of 235.01% and 176.46%, respectively.

Intel Corporation (INTC), despite recent news of a tumble, recorded a substantial 12-month gain of 148.49% and carries a Strong Buy quant rating of 4.93. Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) ranked sixth with a 105.03% gain and also holds a Strong Buy quant rating of 4.81.

Here is the list:

  1. Micron Technology (MU), 12 month percentage change: 263.98% (Quant Rating: Strong Buy 4.99)

  2. Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS), 12 month percentage change: 235.01% (Quant Rating: Buy 4.47)

  3. ACM Research (ACMR), 12 month percentage change: 176.46% (Quant Rating: Hold 3.14)

  4. Lam Research (LRCX), 12 month percentage change: 165.11% (Quant Rating: Hold 3.49)

  5. Intel (INTC), 12 month percentage change: 148.49% (Quant Rating: Strong Buy 4.93)

  6. Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), 12 month percentage change: 105.03% (Quant Rating: Strong Buy 4.81)

  7. Amkor Technology (AMKR), 12 month percentage change: 97.70% (Quant Rating: Strong Buy 4.93)

  8. KLA Corporation (KLAC), 12 month percentage change: 94.32% (Quant Rating: Hold 3.38)

  9. Rambus (RMBS), 12 month percentage change: 93.44% (Quant Rating: Hold 3.28)

  10. FormFactor (FORM), 12 month percentage change: 83.26% (Quant Rating: Hold 3.47)

Semiconductor ETFs: (SMH), (SOXX), (SOXL), (FTXL), (XSD), (USD), (PSI), and (SEMI)

data.title: Insmed a new buy at Roth on Brinsupri and Arikayce sales
data.description:
• Roth has initiated Insmed (INSM) at buy saying it sees above consensus sales of Brinsupri (brensocatib) and Arikayce (amikacin liposome inhalation suspension). 
  • The firm set a $212 price target (~32% upside based on Jan. 22 close).
  • Analyst Adam Walsh sees Brinsupri peak sales of $8.1B and for Arikayce, $1.3B.
  • Walsh also highlighted the potential of treprostinil palmitil inhalation powder (TPIP) for pulmonary arterial hypertension given positive mid-stage trial results released in June 2025. He sees $5B probability of success-adjusted peak sales.

data.title: Microsoft's Fairwater AI data centers likely to boost Azure: UBS
data.description:
• Microsoft's (MSFT) Fairwater AI data centers — which started going online in 2025 — are likely to boost growth for the tech giant's Azure unit, UBS said.
  • “Importantly, we did not pick up evidence of material go-live delays and all partners confirmed that Fairwater Wisconsin would begin going live in early 2026,” UBS analyst Karl Keirstead wrote in a note to clients. “Although estimates varied, the consensus was to bring the data center online in 50 MW chunks, with 100 MW by the end of Feb., 200 MW by the end of March and 400 MW by the end of June, and 500 MW by the middle of the summer.”
  • Keirstead continued: “Bottom line, our work led us to the conclusion that our Azure growth estimates established post the 1Q/Sept print are too conservative, and for the 4Q/Jun 2026 quarter, we’re raising our c/c Azure growth estimate from 35% to 37%. Given the magnitude of GPU capacity coming online throughout 2026, we also conclude that Street capex estimates for FY27 are likely biased up.”
  • Keirstead reiterated his Buy rating on Microsoft, but lowered his price target to $600 from $650.
  • Microsoft is set to report fiscal second-quarter results after the close of trading on Jan. 28.

data.title: Taiwan president supports more semiconductor investment in Arizona: report
data.description:
Taiwan President Lai Ching-te told visiting U.S. Senator Ruben Gallego (D-Ariz.) he supports the investments Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) has made in Arizona and looks forward to more, according to Reuters.

"We look forward to even more semiconductor manufacturing, research and development facilities ⁠springing up in the greater Phoenix area," Ching-te said, according to the report. "This will continue to ‍strengthen Taiwan-U.S. relations." 

TSM has already committed to investing $165B in facilities located around the Arizona capital, the report said. U.S. President Donald Trump has pushed for more semiconductor manufacturing operations within the U.S. and reached a deal with Taiwan last week to reduce export tariffs from Taiwan to a maximum of 15% from 20%.

Taiwanese companies plan to invest a total of at least $250B to "build and expand advanced semiconductor, energy, and artificial intelligence production and innovation capacity in the United States," according to the U.S. Department of Commerce. 

Taiwan is also expected to provide credit guarantees of at least $250B to facilitate additional investment in the expansion of the semiconductor supply chain in the U.S.

data.title: Ulta Beauty expands in the Middle East with a store in the UAE
data.description:
Ulta Beauty (ULTA) announced on Friday that it continued its global expansion with the brand's debut in the United Arab Emirates. The specialty beauty retailer will open a store in Mall of the Emirates on January 29. The store launch is in partnership with Alshaya Group, one of the world’s leading international retail franchise operators. The Mall of the Emirates opening will be closely followed by new Ulta Beauty (ULTA) stores in Dubai Mall and Red Sea Mall in Jeddah.

The company highlighted that the Mall of the Emirates opening introduces Ulta Beauty’s distinctive, all-inclusive beauty destination to the UAE, offering makeup, skin care, hair care, and fragrance, as well as in-store makeup, skin, brow, and nail services delivered by category experts all under one roof.

To mark its debut in the UAE, Ulta Beauty will roll out a dynamic program of pre-launch and launch-week events at Mall of the Emirates, spotlighting regional founders of homegrown beauty brands.

"The UAE launch marks an exciting milestone for Ulta Beauty and Alshaya Group as we continue our expansion in the Middle East and bring our distinctive beauty experience to the region," highlighted Ulta Beauty (ULTA) CEO Kecia Steelman. 

Ulta Beauty (ULTA) first opened a store in the Middle East on November 7 at The Avenues mall in Kuwait.

data.title: Top Quant-rated semiconductor stocks amid Intel's stock decline
data.description:
As Intel's (INTC) weaker-than-expected outlook and supply constraints continued to pull down the shares of the company on Friday, we took a look at chip-related stocks and how they are rated on the Seeking Alpha Quant scale.

Below is a list of the top 10 semiconductor-related stocks ranked according to their Seeking Alpha Quant Ratings. The list includes companies in both the semiconductors sector and the semiconductor materials and equipment industry.

Micron Technology (MU) tops the list with a near-perfect Quant Rating of 4.99. Intel Corporation (INTC) and Amkor Technology (AMKR) follow closely behind, each with a rating of 4.93, while Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) comes in fourth with a rating of 4.81.

All four of these top-ranked companies hold a “Strong Buy” qualitative rating from the Quant system. Navitas Semiconductor Corporation (NVTS) and Onto Innovation Inc. (ONTO) are also among the highly rated stocks on the list, with ratings of 4.47 and 4.26, respectively.

Here is the list:

  1. Micron Technology, Inc. (MU), Quant Rating: 4.99

  2. Intel Corporation (INTC), Quant Rating: 4.93

  3. Amkor Technology, Inc. (AMKR), Quant Rating: 4.93

  4. Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD), Quant Rating: 4.81

  5. Navitas Semiconductor Corporation (NVTS), Quant Rating: 4.47

  6. Onto Innovation Inc. (ONTO), Quant Rating: 4.26

  7. Silicon Laboratories Inc. (SLAB), Quant Rating: 4.12

  8. Ultra Clean Holdings, Inc. (UCTT), Quant Rating: 3.99

  9. Ambarella, Inc. (AMBA), Quant Rating: 3.83

  10. Synaptics Incorporated (SYNA), Quant Rating: 3.81

Semiconductor ETFs: (SMH), (SOXX), (SOXL), (FTXL), (XSD), (USD), (PSI), and (SEMI)

data.title: Philip Morris urges FDA to classify ZYN as modified risk tobacco product
data.description:
Philip Morris International (PM) presented data to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration’s Tobacco Products Scientific Advisory Committee to classify the company’s smokeless tobacco pouch ZYN as a Modified Risk Tobacco Product ((MRTP)).

The MRTP designation would allow Philip Morris (PM) to advertise ZYN as a lower-risk alternative to tobacco, thereby encouraging the switch from cigarettes to nicotine pouches. An MRTP designation for ZYN would be the first granted for a nicotine pouch in the U.S.

“Using ZYN instead of cigarettes puts you at a lower risk of mouth cancer, heart disease, lung cancer, stroke, emphysema, and chronic bronchitis,” the company claimed in their presentation to the FDA.

As part of the FDA’s presentation, the agency stated, "The evidence suggests the proposed modified risk claim is scientifically accurate," adding that “U.S. adult and youth nicotine pouch use prevalence is currently relatively low” and that “nicotine pouch use is most common among adults who recently quit other tobacco products.”

In 2024, Philip Morris (PM) saw a 14% surge in the sale of smoke-free products—including IQOS heated tobacco products—reaching more than $15B with a gross margin that is reportedly six times higher than cigarette sales. The company’s smokeless tobacco business currently contributes at least 40% to Philip Morris’s total net revenue.

In its most recent quarterly results, Philip Morris (PM) reported record quarterly smoke-free gross profit, leading to an increase in its adjusted gross profit outlook for FY25.
